definition: The 1998-2021 negative stock-bond correlation regime was historically anomalous, not the structural norm. Positive correlation governed approximately 48 of 75 post-war years (~64%). The sustained negative correlation coincided with a specific institutional configuration: credible inflation targeting, disinflationary secular forces, massive EM/petrodollar reserve accumulation, and demand-shock-dominated business cycles.
evidence: Positive correlation governed ~64% of the 1950-2025 period (48 of 75 years)
The 1998-2021 negative regime coincided with globalization, favorable demographics, tech disinflation, and EM reserve accumulation
NK model predicts negative correlation under credible inflation targeting — but such conditions are historically unusual
Debate pair_0 called this 'essentially an empirical fact, not an analytical judgment'
open_questions: Is negative correlation the 'natural' state under good policy, or a historical anomaly? Framing disagreement with real portfolio implications
Can the institutional configuration that sustained negative correlation be reconstructed, or are the secular forces irreversibly changed?
